sourceSummary.oneLine: Substantial equivalence discussion comparing the HnX-P1 and HnX-PB portable X-ray system to predicate device TR90BH.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports a 510(k) substantial equivalence rationale by comparing indications, technological characteristics, differences, and performance evidence against a predicate device.
keyPoints: Predicate Device#1 is identified with 510(k) Number K182207, applicant MinXray, Inc., trade/device name TR90BH, regulation name Mobile x-ray system, and classification name System, X-ray, Mobile.
The subject device trade names are HnX-P1 and HnX-PB, with common name Portable X-ray system, product code IZL, regulation number 21 CFR 892.1720, and 510(k) review panel Radiology.
The subject device indications state that the portable x-ray system may be used for diagnostic imaging of body extremities and is not for mammography use.
The document states that differences in weight, size, battery, exposure times, mA, memory settings, kW, kVp, X-ray tube, and collimator do not negatively affect safety and effectiveness.
The document states that battery safety was proven by IEC 62133-2 laboratory test and X-ray tube/collimator differences were verified through IEC 60601-2-28 and IEC 60601-2-54 lab tests.

concepts.name: Substantial Equivalence
concepts.evidence: In comparing the intended use statements, the terminology demonstrates that the subject device has the same indications as the predicates and supports the substantially equivalent determination in this regard.

evidence.claim: The subject device uses a Li-Polymer rechargeable battery rated 29.6Vdc, 1,470mAh.

evidence.claim: The subject device has exposure times from 0.040 to 2.000 seconds in 20 steps.
The subject device mA ratings are 10mA at 50kV to 70kV and 8mA at 71kV to 90kV.

reviewFlags.reason: Document date is not present in the provided source text.
Predicate 510(k) number is conflicting: K182207 is listed in Predicate Device#1 and comparison table, while the decision-making table comment states K203188.
Source text repeatedly uses 'Potable X-ray System'; this may be OCR or source spelling for 'Portable X-ray System' because the body text and common name state 'Portable X-ray system'.
Substantial Equivalence Decision-Making Table checkmark placement is ambiguous in extracted text for Step 4 because the row text shows a checkmark without preserving table column alignment.
